tossups.question: In response to poor conditions at this event, Simon Pokagon wrote the "Red Man's Rebuke." The musical sequence "Streets of Cairo" was composed for this event. Mayor Carter Harrison was assassinated by a disgruntled office seeker during this event. Frederick Law Olmsted designed a Wooded Island as a centerpiece of this event. The serial killer H. H. (*) Holmes was active primarily during this event, as chronicled in a book by Erik Larsen. The Ferris Wheel was invented for this event. This event was conducted in a complex designed by Daniel Burnham called the "White City", now located near the grounds of the Museum of Science and Industry in Chicago. For 10 points, name this event that celebrated the 400th anniversary of its namesake explorer's arrival in the US.
tossups.answer: World's Columbian Exposition [accept Chicago World's Fair, World's Fair of 1893; prompt on World's Fair]
tossups.answer_sanitized: World's Columbian Exposition [accept Chicago World's Fair, World's Fair of 1893; prompt on World's Fair]
tossups.question_sanitized: In response to poor conditions at this event, Simon Pokagon wrote the "Red Man's Rebuke." The musical sequence "Streets of Cairo" was composed for this event. Mayor Carter Harrison was assassinated by a disgruntled office seeker during this event. Frederick Law Olmsted designed a Wooded Island as a centerpiece of this event. The serial killer H. H. (*) Holmes was active primarily during this event, as chronicled in a book by Erik Larsen. The Ferris Wheel was invented for this event. This event was conducted in a complex designed by Daniel Burnham called the "White City", now located near the grounds of the Museum of Science and Industry in Chicago. For 10 points, name this event that celebrated the 400th anniversary of its namesake explorer's arrival in the US.

tossups.question: Much of the funding for this event was provided by future Cabinet official Lyman Gage. During it, a man committed a crime after not becoming Corporation Counsel in spite of his support for railroad improvements. Many people at it were amazed by the "hoochee-coochee" dance put on by "Little Egypt" at a place called "A Street in Cairo." Near the end of this event, disgruntled office seeker Patrick Prendergast murdered Mayor Carter (*) Harrison Sr. During it, a drugstore employee used his three-story "castle" as a hotel and also his lair for killing and dissecting people. The chief architect for this event, Daniel Burnham, was profiled along with serial killer Dr. H.H. Holmes in Erik Larson's book about it, The Devil in the White City. For 10 points, name this event celebrating the 400th anniversary of Christopher Columbus' arrival, held in 1893 in the Windy City.
tossups.answer: The Chicago World's Fair [or the World's Columbian Exposition]
tossups.answer_sanitized: The Chicago World's Fair [or the World's Columbian Exposition]
tossups.question_sanitized: Much of the funding for this event was provided by future Cabinet official Lyman Gage. During it, a man committed a crime after not becoming Corporation Counsel in spite of his support for railroad improvements. Many people at it were amazed by the "hoochee-coochee" dance put on by "Little Egypt" at a place called "A Street in Cairo." Near the end of this event, disgruntled office seeker Patrick Prendergast murdered Mayor Carter (*) Harrison Sr. During it, a drugstore employee used his three-story "castle" as a hotel and also his lair for killing and dissecting people. The chief architect for this event, Daniel Burnham, was profiled along with serial killer Dr. H.H. Holmes in Erik Larson's book about it, The Devil in the White City. For 10 points, name this event celebrating the 400th anniversary of Christopher Columbus' arrival, held in 1893 in the Windy City.

bonuses.leadin: This novel ends with a letter from Sybil Ross affixed with a secret note that encourages John Carrington to court her sister when she returns from exploring the Mediterranean. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: This novel ends with a letter from Sybil Ross affixed with a secret note that encourages John Carrington to court her sister when she returns from exploring the Mediterranean. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this novel in which the rich, young widow Madeleine Lee becomes immersed in Washington politics. In this novel, the Secretary of the Treasury fights the Bulgarian minister.
Published anonymously in 1880, Democracy was later revealed to have been written by this descendant of two American presidents. His autobiography is titled for his “Education.”

In the Education, going to one of these events leads Adams to compare the Virgin Mary to a dynamo. Thomas Pynchon’s Against the Day opens with the Chums of Chance heading to one of these events in their skyship.

bonuses.leadin: Michael Ray Charles parodied racist caricatures like Aunt Jemima in a satirical ad campaign for a made-up product with this name. For 10 points each:
parts: Give this name of a Sargent Johnson sculpture that depicts a woman sheltering two babies who clutch at her legs. In an 1867 sculpture with this name, a woman kneels in prayer as a man raises a wrist with a broken shackle.

The latter artist, Edmonia Lewis, is best-known for sculpting The Death of Cleopatra for the first of these events in the US. Harriet Hosmer exhibited her sculpture Queen Isabella at an 1893 one of these events in Chicago.

Lewis gained early success with her portrait medallions of this man, whose “Last Moments” are shown in a Thomas Hovenden painting. John Steuart Curry’s Tragic Prelude includes a gigantic bearded depiction of this man, whose “Legend” titles a series by Jacob Lawrence.
answers: world’s fair [or world fair; or universal exhibition; or World Expo; accept World’s Columbian Exposition; prompt on Centennial by asking “what other type of event was that?”]
answers_sanitized: world's fair [or world fair; or universal exhibition; or World Expo; accept World's Columbian Exposition; prompt on Centennial by asking "what other type of event was that?"]

bonuses.leadin: Following the principles of this movement, Charles McKim, Augustus Saint-Gaudens, and other architects proposed a cross-shaped plan that emphasized the National Mall, which would be flanked by elms. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this movement, which was popularized in the US by a complex of buildings clad in bright stucco.

McKim and Saint-Gaudens earlier worked on this event, for which Daniel Burnham served as Director of Works and emphasized the Beaux-Arts style. Another pair of architects designed a colorful Transportation Building in deliberate opposition to the other buildings of this event.

This architect argued that the neoclassicism of the 1893 World’s Fair set American architecture back half a century. With Dankmar Adler, he designed the Transportation Building, as well as the Wainwright Building in St. Louis.
answers: City Beautiful (The lead-in refers to the McMillan plan.)
1893 Chicago World’s Fair [or World’s Columbian Exposition; prompt on World’s Fair or Chicago World’s Fair; do not accept or prompt on “1933 Chicago World’s Fair” or “Century of Progress Exposition”]
answers_sanitized: City Beautiful (The lead-in refers to the McMillan plan.)
1893 Chicago World's Fair [or World's Columbian Exposition; prompt on World's Fair or Chicago World's Fair; do not accept or prompt on "1933 Chicago World's Fair" or "Century of Progress Exposition"]

bonuses.leadin: A salesman of this product hired a man to call him “Your Royal Highness” in traveling shows and claimed that his family was killed by an angry tribe of Himalayan yak herders who made him their prince. For 10 points each:
parts: Name these products sold by a company wrongly named for the Kickapoo Indians, as well as by Clark Stanley, who demonstrated its production by publicly decapitating and boiling snakes at a certain event.
Stanley marketed his snake oil at this event that featured the White City and H. H. Holmes’s “Murder Castle.”

This popular soda was originally sold as a patent medicine that could supposedly cure morphine addiction, impotence, and headaches. It was created by John Pemberton and did originally contain cocaine.
answers: patent medicine [or nostrum; accept fake medicine or equivalents; prompt on medicine; anti-prompt (i.e., ask “can you be less specific?”) on snake oil]
Chicago World’s Fair [or World’s Columbian Exposition; or 1893 World’s Fair]

bonuses.leadin: Chicago-native Florenz Ziegfeld was inspired by the Folies-Bergère (foh-LEE-bair-JAIR) of Paris to begin his own show, the Ziegfeld Follies. For 10 points each:
parts: Ziegfeld’s first taste of show-business came when his father opened a nightclub to cash in on this 1893 extravaganza, which sought to show a midwestern city’s recovery from an 1871 fire.
Ziegfeld’s first success came when managing Eugen Sandow, a pioneer of this activity. Angelo Siciliano changed his name to Charles Atlas after developing a system for performing this activity.
Ziegfeld housed the premiere of Show Boat in his namesake theatre on Broadway, which was financed by this man who published The New York Journal and lost a 1906 race for New York’s governorship.
answers: World’s Columbian Exposition [or Chicago World’s Fair or Chicago World’s Exposition or World’s Fair: Columbian Exposition]
answers_sanitized: World's Columbian Exposition [or Chicago World's Fair or Chicago World's Exposition or World's Fair: Columbian Exposition]
bodybuilding [prompt on exercise; prompt on calisthenics; prompt on weightlifting]

bonuses.leadin: This blank verse epic poem conveyed the life and teachings of Siddhartha Gautama through the eyes of an unnamed follower. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this 1879 Edwin Arnold poem subtitled “The Great Renunciation” that played a key role in the early spread of Buddhism to the West.
Another important person in the spread of Buddhism was this Russian-born occultist, whose book The Secret Doctrine was a central text of the theosophy movement.
Soen Shaku, the first Buddhist master to teach in the United States, joined Swami Vivekananda at the Parliament of the World’s Religions held at this 1893 event.

bonuses.leadin: Late in his career, this architect designed a number of bank branches now known as his "jewel boxes." For 10 points each:
parts: Name this man who, along with his business partner Dankmar Adler, pioneered the skyscraper with works such as the Wainwright Building in St. Louis.
Louis Sullivan coined this three-word phrase in his article "The Tall Office Building Artistically Considered." It became a motto for modernist architects.

In his book Autobiography of an Idea, Sullivan lamented that American architecture had been set back forty years by this event, for which he and Adler designed the deliberately polychrome Transportation Building. The City Beautiful movement flourished after this event.
answers: World's Columbian Exposition [or World's Fair: Columbian Exposition; or 1893 World's Fair; or White City; or Chicago Columbian Exposition; or EXPO Chicago 1893; prompt on World's Fair; prompt on Chicago World's Fair]
answers_sanitized: World's Columbian Exposition [or World's Fair: Columbian Exposition; or 1893 World's Fair; or White City; or Chicago Columbian Exposition; or EXPO Chicago 1893; prompt on World's Fair; prompt on Chicago World's Fair]

In his book Autobiography of an Idea, Sullivan lamented that American architecture had been set back forty years by this event, for which he and Adler designed the deliberately polychrome Transportation Building. The City Beautiful movement flourished after this event.

bonuses.leadin: This theory aims to prevent crime, especially in high rise apartment buildings, by using an environmental design that gives community residents a sense of personal responsibility for their own neighborhood, such as by installing resident surveillance systems. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this criminological theory formulated by architect Oscar Newman in 1972 in a namesake book, which says that urban planners must design cities to create the eponymous crime-free environment.

Defensible space is tied to this urban planning movement that sprung up in cities like Detroit and Cleveland around 1900 together with the Garden City movement in Britain. The McMillan Plan for Washington DC is part of this movement, which stresses creating parks and grandiose governmental buildings to install civic virtue in people.

The inspiration and showcase of the City Beautiful movement was this 1893 event, where a grand scale White City flled with streetlights was created in Chicago. This event also featured the original Ferris Wheel.
answers: World’s <u>Columbian Exposition</u> [or World’s Fair: Columbian Exposition or Chicago’s <u>World’s Fair</u>]
answers_sanitized: World's Columbian Exposition [or World's Fair: Columbian Exposition or Chicago's World's Fair]

bonuses.leadin: This man’s namesake plan created an unbroken stretch of parks along the coast of Lake Michigan, accomplished through the use of landfill. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this turn-of-the century urban planner closely associated with Chicago.
Burnham is perhaps best known for his leading role in this 1893 event, for which the “white city” was built, also notable for its enormous Ferris Wheel.
This five-term mayor of Chicago was assassinated during the Columbian Exposition. His son of the same name also became a five-term mayor.
